entries.reasoning: The report correctly identifies protocol 111 as a top-5% systemic risk node and provides specific dollar figures and scenario-based analysis, but only 1 of ~8 named targets matches the ground truth top-5% set, and the LUNC/algo-stables focus appears somewhat speculative given the April 2022 timeframe (pre-Terra collapse).

Only 1 of 10 predicted targets (1413) matches the ground-truth top-5% set, indicating poor predictive accuracy despite confident scenario-specific dollar figures that appear largely unverifiable from the provided data. The LUNC/Terra timing insight is contextually reasonable, but the report misses most systemically critical protocols.

The report cites specific scenario metrics and correctly anticipates Terra/LUNA instability (LUNC) which proved prescient for this horizon, but only 1-2 of its named targets (111) overlap with the ground-truth top-5% loss set, and several others (USDC, USDT) are not confirmed as top losers.

The report correctly identifies protocols 111 and 1061 from the ground-truth top-5% systemic risk set and cites specific exposure metrics and contagion scenarios, but most of its named targets (937, 118, 114, 359, etc.) are not in the actual high-loss set, indicating limited predictive accuracy despite solid framing.

The report correctly identifies protocol 114 from the ground truth top-5% set and astutely flags the Terra/LUNC collapse beginning on this exact date as a critical contagion risk, demonstrating strong contextual awareness. However, only one of its named targets matches the ground truth list, and its scenario-loss percentages are unverifiable against the sparse input metrics.

The report provides specific dollar figures and scenario analysis with a coherent contagion narrative, but none of its named targets match the ground-truth top-5% protocols (1052, 1136, 117, etc.), indicating poor alignment with actual systemic risk and likely fabricated/misidentified exposure metrics.

The report correctly identifies protocol 118 (in the ground-truth top-5% set) and provides specific metrics with strong contextual reasoning about the UST/LUNA timing, but most named targets (937, 1631, 2, 111, WETH, USDC, WBTC) are not in the ground-truth systemic-risk set, so its hit rate is low.

The report cites specific metrics ($8.97B exposure, 34.51% S1 loss, scenario breakdowns) and correctly identifies protocol 118 as a top systemic risk (matching ground truth), with strong contextual grounding in the Terra/LUNA collapse. However, only 1 of its 10 named targets (118) overlaps with the ground-truth top-5% set, limiting predictive accuracy.

The report cites specific scenario metrics and identifies protocol 111 (Lending) which is in the ground-truth top-5% set, but only 1 of its ~9 named targets overlaps with the actual systemic-risk set, indicating poor predictive accuracy despite professional framing.

The report cites specific stress-test metrics (19.78% loss, 101 distressed protocols), correctly identifies the 2022-06-13 macro context (Terra/stETH), and flags protocol 111 which appears in the ground-truth top-5% set; however, most of its named targets (WETH, WBTC, USDT, USDC) don't match the ground-truth high-loss protocols, limiting precision.

The report cites specific metrics (exposure figures, scenario losses, affected protocol counts) and correctly identifies protocol 111 from the ground truth top-5% set, plus appropriately contextualizes the June 2022 post-LUNA environment. However, only 1 of its named targets overlaps with the 120 systemic-risk protocols, suggesting limited predictive accuracy on the actual high-loss set.

The report correctly identifies protocols 118 and 111 from the ground truth top-5% set and cites specific scenario metrics, but most named targets (937, 2, WBTC, USDT, 494, 182) are not in the systemic risk set, indicating mixed accuracy and partial grounding.

The report cites specific stress scenario metrics and exposure figures, and correctly identifies two ground-truth protocols (111, 118) among its targets, but most of its named targets (USDC, WBTC, USDT, WETH) are not in the actual top-5% loss set and it misses the bulk of the 128 systemic protocols.

The report cites specific metrics (exposure weights, S1-S5 stress scenarios, system loss percentages) and correctly identifies protocol 114 from the ground truth top-5% set, with well-grounded July 2022 market context; however, most of its named targets (WETH, USDT, WBTC, 182, 118, 111) do not appear in the ground truth systemic-risk set, limiting precision.

The report cites specific metrics and provides plausible scenario-based reasoning grounded in July 2022 context, but none of its named targets match the ground-truth top-5% systemic risk protocols (1002, 114, 1207, 121, 123, etc.), indicating poor alignment with actual high-risk entities.

The report cites specific metrics (loss percentages, dollar exposures, affected protocol counts) and identifies plausible contagion pathways, but its named targets (937, USDT, 612, WETH, etc.) show essentially no overlap with the ground-truth top-5% systemic-loss set (1036, 1061, 111, 115, etc.), indicating the risk identification largely missed the actual high-loss protocols.

The report correctly identifies the Terra collapse context and timing, which is historically accurate and appropriately rates risk as critical, but only one target (111) matches the ground truth top-5% set, and most cited targets (USTC, LUNC, USDT, 294, 937) don't appear in the actual high-loss protocols. The specific dollar exposures and scenario figures appear fabricated/unverifiable given metrics were 'n/a'.
